evals.prompt: I manage a coding bootcamp and want to auto-grade student project submissions. Each project should be scored on code correctness, code readability, and documentation quality on a 1-5 scale. Correctness should count for 50%, readability 30%, and docs 20%. I have about 40 projects that two instructors have already graded by hand. Can you build a scorer that matches our instructors' grading and flags anything where the AI disagrees significantly?
evals.expected_output: A Python script that defines a DSPy rubric scorer with three weighted criteria, scores each criterion independently using ChainOfThought, includes anchor examples for calibration, validates scores with dspy.Assert, compares against gold-standard human grades using a MAE or agreement metric, and optimizes with MIPROv2 or BootstrapFewShot.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature for scoring a single criterion

assertions.name: scores_per_criterion
assertions.description: Scores each criterion in a separate call to avoid halo effect

assertions.name: uses_chain_of_thought
assertions.description: Uses ChainOfThought so the model reasons before assigning a score

assertions.name: includes_weights
assertions.description: Applies the specified weights (50/30/20) when computing overall score

assertions.name: includes_anchor_examples
assertions.description: Provides calibration anchors showing what different score levels look like

assertions.name: uses_assert_for_validation
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Assert to enforce valid score ranges

assertions.name: includes_evaluation_metric
assertions.description: Defines a metric (MAE or agreement) comparing AI scores to human gold scores

assertions.name: includes_optimization
assertions.description: Uses a DSPy optimizer to improve scorer accuracy against the metric

evals.prompt: Our customer success team reviews 200 support conversations per week for quality. We score each on helpfulness, accuracy, and tone — all equally weighted on a 1-5 scale. Right now it takes 3 hours a week. Can you build an AI auditor that scores these automatically and gives a pass/needs_coaching decision? A conversation passes if the overall score is 3.5 or above.
evals.expected_output: A Python script with a DSPy module that scores support conversations on three criteria independently, computes a weighted overall score, and makes a pass/needs_coaching decision based on a 3.5 threshold. Should require justifications citing evidence from the conversation.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature for scoring support conversations

assertions.name: scores_per_criterion
assertions.description: Scores helpfulness, accuracy, and tone in separate calls

assertions.name: requires_justification
assertions.description: Each score includes a justification field citing conversation evidence

assertions.name: includes_threshold_decision
assertions.description: Implements pass/needs_coaching logic based on the 3.5 threshold

assertions.name: uses_chain_of_thought
assertions.description: Uses ChainOfThought for reasoning before scoring

evals.prompt: We are evaluating vendor proposals for a procurement process. Each proposal needs to be scored on technical fit, cost competitiveness, and team experience. The stakes are high so I want multiple independent AI raters and I want to flag any proposal where the raters disagree by more than 1 point on any criterion. Can you build an ensemble scorer?
evals.expected_output: A Python script with an ensemble scoring module that runs 3 independent raters on each proposal, averages scores, detects disagreements above 1 point spread per criterion, and flags proposals needing human review.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_module
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Module for the ensemble scorer

assertions.name: multiple_independent_raters
assertions.description: Creates 3 or more independent scorer instances

assertions.name: detects_disagreement
assertions.description: Computes per-criterion score spread and flags disagreements above 1 point

assertions.name: flags_for_human_review
assertions.description: Returns a flag indicating whether the proposal needs human review

assertions.name: averages_scores
assertions.description: Computes averaged overall score across raters
